name: A Total War Saga: TROY
synopsis: Set during the mythological Trojan War, TROY blends historical strategy with Greek legend as you command armies across the Aegean. Players choose from iconic heroes like Achilles and Hector, each with unique mechanics, managing resources, diplomacy and epic real-time battles. It was initially free on Epic Games Store at launch, making it one of gaming's most downloaded titles.

name: Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition
synopsis: The definitive remaster of one of the greatest RTS games ever made, featuring all expansions plus new civilizations, a 4K visual overhaul, and remastered soundtrack. Players build civilizations from the Dark Age to the Imperial Age across 35+ campaigns spanning history from the Britons to the Persians. Its active multiplayer community and mod support have kept it thriving for 25 years.

name: Age of Empires IV
synopsis: A modern reimagining of the classic RTS franchise, set across four major historical periods with eight distinct civilizations each featuring asymmetric mechanics. Developed by Relic Entertainment, it introduces tactical documentary overlays, a revamped siege system, and deep campaign storytelling. The game balances accessibility for newcomers with deep strategic options for veterans.

name: Age of Mythology: Retold
synopsis: A full remake of the beloved 2002 mythology-based RTS, letting players command armies backed by gods from Greek, Egyptian, Norse and Atlantean pantheons. Each civilization worships different gods granting powerful mythological units and divine powers that shift the tide of battle. The Retold edition brings fully remodelled visuals, reworked balance, and optional cross-play.

name: Anno 1800
synopsis: A city-building and economic strategy game set during the Industrial Revolution, where players build sprawling metropolises, manage intricate production chains, and conduct diplomacy across multiple islands. Its sandbox campaign layers trade routes, factory automation and exploration voyages into a richly textured simulation. Anno 1800 received extensive post-launch DLC support making it one of the deepest entries in the franchise.

name: Baldur's Gate 3
synopsis: An epic turn-based RPG set in the Forgotten Realms using D&D 5th Edition rules, where a group of heroes infected by mind flayer parasites must uncover a dark conspiracy threatening all of Faerûn. Featuring unparalleled reactivity, over 170 hours of content, and a cast of brilliantly written companions, it redefines what a CRPG can be. It swept virtually every Game of the Year award for 2023.

name: Battlefield 3
synopsis: DICE's landmark military shooter set across global conflicts including Tehran, Paris and New York, featuring the Frostbite 2 engine with destructible environments. The multiplayer introduced Conquest and Rush modes that defined a generation of FPS games, with 64-player battles across massive maps. Its single-player follows a US Marine investigating a PLR terrorist plot.

name: Chivalry 2
synopsis: A first-person medieval melee multiplayer game featuring 64-player team objective battles across castles, villages and siege scenarios. Players hack, slash and block with swords, axes and polearms using a fluid directional combat system that rewards timing and aggression. It is notorious for its chaotic screaming-charge gameplay and dark comedy.

name: Cities: Skylines
synopsis: The definitive modern city-builder that dethroned SimCity, offering tools to design road networks, zone districts, and manage utilities across sprawling metropolitan areas. Its depth comes from realistic traffic simulation, budget management and citizen happiness systems that make every city a unique puzzle. A massive modding community on Steam Workshop has produced thousands of assets and gameplay overhauls.

name: Company of Heroes 2
synopsis: A brutally realistic WWII real-time strategy game set on the Eastern Front, where Soviet forces battle the Wehrmacht across frozen Russian landscapes with dynamic weather that affects unit movement and morale. The TrueSight line-of-sight system and destructible cover make each engagement tense and tactical. Its multiplayer remains active and includes multiple faction expansions.

name: Control Ultimate Edition
synopsis: A third-person action game set in the brutalist Federal Bureau of Control, where Jesse Faden wields telekinetic powers and a shape-shifting service weapon to battle a paranormal invasion called the Hiss. Remedy Entertainment crafts a deeply strange Metroidvania-style world of shifting architecture and government conspiracies inspired by the SCP Foundation. The Ultimate Edition includes both major DLC expansions.

name: Disco Elysium: The Final Cut
synopsis: A groundbreaking detective RPG with no combat where skill checks, dialogue and internal monologues from the 24 fragmented voices in your head determine how you rebuild an amnesiac cop in a post-revolutionary harbour town. The writing is extraordinary, political and deeply funny, covering alcoholism, ideology, and failure in ways no other game has. It won four BAFTA awards and is widely considered among the greatest games ever made.

name: Far Cry Primal
synopsis: Set in 10,000 BC during the Stone Age, players tame animals, craft weapons from bone and wood, and fight rival tribes to reclaim their homeland in a prehistoric open world. The absence of firearms is replaced by spears, bows and fire, making combat rawer and more primal than the main series. Players can tame predators like sabretooth tigers and cave bears as companions.

name: Gears Tactics
synopsis: A turn-based tactics game set 12 years before the original Gears of War, following Gabe Diaz as he assembles a squad to hunt a monstrous Locust general. It adapts XCOM-style grid combat to the Gears universe with the franchise's signature aggression and gore, rewarding offensive play with extra action points. Its boss encounters are some of the most spectacular set-pieces in the tactics genre.

name: Gears of War 4
synopsis: The fifth entry in the Gears franchise following JD Fenix, son of Marcus Fenix, as a new generation of soldiers face a terrifying new enemy called the Swarm that has evolved from the Locust Horde. It retains the iconic cover-based shooting and co-op Horde mode while adding new enemy types and a gripping father-son narrative. The game launched alongside the Xbox Play Anywhere initiative.

name: Ghost Recon Breakpoint
synopsis: An open-world tactical shooter set on the fictional island of Auroa, where an elite US spec-ops soldier must survive behind enemy lines against rogue Ghosts led by Cole D. Walker. Players can approach missions stealthily or with overwhelming force across a vast island of deserts, jungles and mountain ranges. Post-launch updates added an Immersive Mode removing RPG elements to restore the tactical feel.

name: Grand Theft Auto IV
synopsis: Niko Bellic's immigration story in a bleak rain-soaked Liberty City is one of gaming's most mature and thematically rich narratives, exploring the broken promises of the American Dream. The game introduced a physics engine, cover shooting and a character-driven story that set a new standard for open world storytelling. Its PC port was notorious for performance issues at launch but patched over time.

name: Hearts of Iron IV
synopsis: Paradox's grand strategy wargame covering the entire World War II era, letting players guide any nation through diplomacy, military doctrine, industrial planning and battle. The game's depth is staggering — players design tank divisions, negotiate political alliances, and manage supply lines across the entire globe. An enormous modding community has produced alternate history scenarios spanning Kaiserreich to The New Order.

name: High On Life
synopsis: A first-person shooter written by the Rick and Morty team where your weapons are sentient talking aliens who comment on everything you do, as you hunt a drug cartel that wants to get high on humanity. The game's humour is deeply self-aware and divisive — wall-to-wall meta-commentary and a surreal alien world. Despite polarising reviews it was the biggest Xbox Game Pass launch of 2022.

name: Hitman 2
synopsis: Agent 47 travels to six stunning global locations — Miami, Mumbai, New Zealand and beyond — to assassinate targets in gloriously creative ways within vast sandbox levels. The game rewards replaying missions to discover hundreds of disguise routes, poison setups, and accidental-death opportunities unique to each target. It carries forward all Hitman 2016 missions making it the definitive stealth sandbox.

name: Hogwarts Legacy
synopsis: Set in the 1800s Wizarding World, players attend Hogwarts as a fifth-year student who discovers they hold the key to an ancient secret threatening the magical world. The open-world map includes Hogsmeade, Hogwarts castle and the Scottish Highlands packed with puzzles, beast sanctuaries and ancient magic combat. It became the best-selling game of 2023 in the UK and US within its launch window.

name: Humankind
synopsis: Amplitude Studios' answer to Civilization lets players combine 60 historical cultures across six eras to create a unique civilisation — mixing Vikings with Egyptians or Romans with Mongols. Its fame-based victory system rewards cultural achievement and military prowess equally. It features one of the most scientifically accurate historical representations in any strategy game.

name: Insurgency: Sandstorm
synopsis: A hardcore tactical shooter with near-zero HUD, demanding players rely on real communication, cover and suppression to survive against human opponents across Middle Eastern environments. Its audio design — with directional gunfire, radio chatter, and the crack of bullets — creates genuine fear. Free post-launch updates have added maps, modes and cosmetics over five years.

name: Just Cause 4
synopsis: Rico Rodriguez returns in his most physics-powered adventure yet, with a grappling hook system, infinite tethers and a wingsuit letting players attach rockets to anything in a South American archipelago. The game leans hard into emergent chaos — creating tornados, building flying tanks, and tearing apart environments in spectacular ways. Critics noted the story was thin but the sandbox freedom was unmatched.

name: L.A. Noire
synopsis: Set in 1947 Los Angeles, Detective Cole Phelps rises through the LAPD solving murders, arson and vice cases using the revolutionary MotionScan facial animation technology to detect suspects' lies. The game reconstructs post-war LA in stunning period detail with a jazz-noir soundtrack and a narrative inspired by James Ellroy. Its interrogation system remains one of gaming's most original mechanics.

name: Manor Lords
synopsis: A solo-developed medieval city builder and real-time strategy game where players organically grow a 14th century village into a fortified manor town, managing food, trade and military readiness. Its organic road-placement and granular supply chain simulation set it apart from genre peers. It sold over 1 million copies in its first three days on Steam Early Access in 2024.

name: Max Payne 3
synopsis: Max Payne is now a burned-out mercenary in São Paulo protecting a billionaire's family from Brazilian street gangs in Rockstar's cinematic action masterpiece. The bullet-time shooting system reaches its mechanical peak here with seamless dive-and-shoot set-pieces set to a stunning HEALTH soundtrack. The single-player runs nearly 12 hours of pure cinematic action.

name: Mount & Blade II: Bannerlord
synopsis: A medieval sandbox RPG where players build a mercenary company and conquer the fractured continent of Calradia through politics, trade and enormous real-time battles of hundreds of soldiers. The game blends first-person mounted combat, kingdom management and procedural campaign events into an unstructured freeform experience. After years of Early Access it launched fully in 2022 with a robust modding scene.

name: Need for Speed: Most Wanted
synopsis: The definitive open-world arcade racer set in Fairhaven City where players smash police pursuits and take down a Blacklist of elite street racers to become the most wanted driver in the city. Criterion Games brought their Burnout DNA to create a game where any car on the map can be commandeered and every crash is a work of art. Its seamless open world and drop-in multiplayer were genre-defining in 2012.

name: Old World
synopsis: Designed by Civilization IV lead designer Soren Johnson, Old World brings a dynastic twist to 4X strategy as your ruler ages, has children, and navigates court intrigue across ancient history from Assyria to Rome. Orders are a limited resource that forces agonising choices each turn between military expansion, family drama and cultural development. It is one of the most praised 4X games of the 2020s.

name: Planet Coaster
synopsis: Frontier's theme park construction simulator lets players build roller coasters of impossible complexity using a flexible track tool that produces genuinely terrifying rides. Beyond coasters, players manage staff, guest happiness, finances and scenery in a vibrant sandbox. The game's Blueprint system lets the community share entire parks and ride designs.

name: Planet Zoo
synopsis: A zoo management simulation with the most sophisticated animal behaviour AI in any game, where each of 90+ species has individual needs, personalities and genetic traits that players must manage to maintain welfare and breed conservation populations. Planet Zoo is considered the pinnacle of the management simulation genre with its museum-quality habitat building tools.

name: Psychonauts 2
synopsis: Tim Schafer's long-awaited sequel follows psychic spy Razputin Aquato infiltrating the minds of troubled Psychonauts agents, each with a mental world that visualises their trauma, phobias and memories as platforming levels of extraordinary imagination. It won multiple awards for its empathetic portrayal of mental health and is one of the most acclaimed platformers of the decade.

name: Resident Evil 7 Biohazard
synopsis: A radical first-person reinvention of Resident Evil that strips the series back to pure survival horror, placing Ethan Winters in a derelict Louisiana plantation held by a family of infected cannibalistic hillbillies. Resource scarcity, grotesque monster design and unrelenting tension make it one of the scariest games ever made. Its full VR support in PSVR created legendary horror experiences.

name: Rise of Nations: Extended Edition
synopsis: Brian Reynolds' masterpiece RTS spanning 6,000 years of history from the Ancient Age to the Information Age, combining Civilization-style territory control with real-time base building and combat. Its Conquer the World campaign mode wraps multiplayer battles in a Risk-like strategic layer. The Extended Edition added graphical upgrades and Steam multiplayer to this 2003 classic.

name: RoboCop: Rogue City
synopsis: A first-person shooter set directly between RoboCop 2 and 3, where Detroit's most iconic law enforcement officer investigates a city-wide gang war while confronting his own humanity. The game nails the tone of the original films with slow deliberate movement, bone-crunching gunplay and genuinely clever police work side-quests. Fans called it the most faithful video game adaptation of an 80s action movie ever made.

name: Ryse: Son of Rome
synopsis: Crytek's launch title for Xbox One follows Roman centurion Marius Titus on a campaign of vengeance from the Roman legions in Britain to the Colosseum of Rome, featuring melee combat built around execution chains. Visually it was extraordinary for 2013 and one of the best-looking launch titles in console history. Its single-player campaign tells a moving father-son story set against spectacular Roman spectacle.

name: Scarlet Nexus
synopsis: A stylish action RPG set in a future where humans have evolved psychokinetic powers and a brain-worshipping organisation defends Earth from grotesque Psynet mutants called Others. Players switch between two protagonists — Yuito and Kasane — with completely different stories that interweave and contradict each other. Its anime-inspired visuals, fast combo combat and psychological bond system make it a standout JRPG.

name: Shadow Tactics: Blades of the Shogun
synopsis: A hardcore real-time tactics game set in Edo-period Japan where players command five specialists — samurai, ninja, sniper, thief and child — to eliminate enemies using patience, timing and environmental traps. Each character has unique abilities that must be combined to solve handcrafted levels. It is widely regarded as one of the finest tactical games ever made and spawned the Desperados series.

name: Sid Meier's Civilization V
synopsis: The fifth iteration of Firaxis' legendary 4X franchise introduced hexagonal tiles, one-unit-per-tile combat and a city-state diplomacy system that fundamentally changed strategy gaming. Players lead a historical civilisation from prehistory to the space age through culture, science, faith, diplomacy or conquest. With its Brave New World and Gods & Kings expansions it is considered the peak of the franchise by many fans.

name: Sid Meier's Civilization VII
synopsis: The seventh mainline entry in the legendary 4X series introduces a revolutionary Age system that resets the map and civilisation choices three times per game, forcing strategic pivots. For the first time, leaders and civilisations are decoupled, letting players pair historical figures with any culture. Early reception praised the innovation but noted interface and content gaps at launch.

name: Sniper Elite 3
synopsis: Set in WWII North Africa, players take long-range shots against German targets using authentic ballistics that account for wind, gravity and heartbeat in a tactical sandbox. The signature X-ray kill cam renders every successful shot in gruesome slow-motion anatomical detail. Players can approach objectives stealthily or initiate firefights across open desert environments with multiple route options.

name: Stellaris
synopsis: Paradox's space 4X grand strategy game lets players design a custom alien civilisation and guide it from first contact to galactic supremacy across a procedurally generated universe of stars and anomalies. The game's event chains and emergent crises — from ancient fallen empires to extradimensional horrors — create stories unlike any scripted campaign. A decade of expansions has built one of the deepest strategy games ever made.

name: Terra Invicta
synopsis: A near-future grand strategy game where seven ideologically opposed factions compete to control Earth and colonise the solar system in response to an alien invasion. Players manage national politics, research space technology, build orbital stations and deploy agents in an extraordinarily complex simulation that treats spaceflight with hard science accuracy. It is considered the most ambitious indie strategy game of the 2020s.

name: Total War: SHOGUN 2
synopsis: Creative Assembly's critically acclaimed return to feudal Japan, where rival daimyos fight to unite Japan under their banner through political intrigue, siege warfare and samurai blade combat. The focused Japanese setting gives it a coherent aesthetic and thematic identity that later entries struggled to match. Its Fall of the Samurai expansion adds a stunning Meiji Restoration era with gunpowder revolutionising the battlefield.

name: Total War: THREE KINGDOMS
synopsis: Set during China's legendary Three Kingdoms era, players choose from historical or semi-mythological warlords to reunify China through diplomacy, assassination and massive real-time battles. The Romance mode treats heroes as near-supernatural warriors who duel on the battlefield, while the Records mode plays as strict historical simulation. Its diplomacy system was the most sophisticated in Total War history at launch.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER II
synopsis: The second entry in Creative Assembly's Warhammer trilogy brings four fantasy races — High Elves, Dark Elves, Lizardmen and Skaven — battling across the New World in a race to control the Great Vortex. Owners of the first game unlock a combined Mortal Empires campaign of unprecedented size. It is widely considered the finest Total War game ever made.

name: Transport Fever 2
synopsis: A transport management simulation spanning 1850 to the present day where players build railway networks, bus routes and shipping lines to connect cities and grow their transport empire. Its detailed train models, realistic terrain deformation tools and organic city growth simulation appeal to enthusiasts. The modding community has added hundreds of real-world vehicles and maps.

name: Tribes of Midgard
synopsis: A Viking-themed action survival game where up to 10 players defend their village's world tree from nightly waves of enemies and giant Jötunn during a 15-day countdown to Ragnarök. Players gather resources, craft gear and explore procedurally generated biomes by day while building defences and fighting invasions by night. Its seasonal content model brought fresh biomes and enemies each quarter.

name: Tropico 6
synopsis: Players rule a fictional Caribbean island nation as El Presidente, balancing the demands of factions — communists, capitalists, the military and the church — while building their banana republic across multiple islands. Its satirical tone skewers political systems with sharp humour as players build bridges, steal world wonders, and suppress or appease their citizens. The archipelago multi-island structure expands on all previous entries.

name: Two Point Campus
synopsis: The spiritual successor to Theme Hospital tasks players with building and managing a series of increasingly bizarre universities — from a culinary school to a wizarding academy — balancing student needs, staff morale and course content. Its wit and charm match the management depth, with a campaign that escalates in wonderfully absurd directions. It was released day one on Game Pass.

name: Victoria 3
synopsis: Paradox's long-awaited sequel to their beloved Victorian-era grand strategy game simulates the economic and social transformation of the 19th century, letting players industrialise nations, abolish slavery, navigate colonialism and manage pop-level citizen happiness. Its economic simulation is the most detailed Paradox has ever made. A heavily modded and patched game that grew significantly post-launch.

name: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate - Daemonhunters
synopsis: A XCOM-style tactics game where players command Grey Knights — the most elite psyker warriors of the Imperium — to hunt a galaxy-threatening plague across procedurally generated starships and plague worlds. Grey Knights level up through a deep class tree and can be customised with psychic powers and wargear. It is considered the finest Warhammer 40K tactics game ever made.

name: Watch Dogs: Legion
synopsis: Set in a near-future dystopian London under authoritarian surveillance, players recruit any NPC in the city as a playable operative for the hacker resistance DedSec. The Play as Anyone system gives each recruit unique skills, backstories and voices — a lorry driver fights differently than a hitman or a beekeeper. The system creates emergent stories unique to each player's run.

name: Workers & Resources: Soviet Republic
synopsis: The most complex city builder ever made, simulating an entire Soviet-era economy from raw ore extraction through processing, manufacturing and distribution to consumer goods with real-time train, truck and power grid simulation. Players must physically lay cables, pipes and roads with exact precision. Its Early Access community is fiercely dedicated and it rivals real logistics software in depth.

name: A Total War Saga: THRONES OF BRITANNIA
synopsis: A focused Total War experience set during the Viking Age, following the fragmentation of Britain after Alfred the Great's death. Players choose from ten kingdoms competing to forge a unified Britain through campaign politics, battlefield command and territorial expansion. Its condensed scope makes it one of the most accessible Total War entries for newcomers.

name: Age of Empires III: Definitive Edition
synopsis: The definitive edition of Ensemble Studios' colonial-era RTS featuring all expansions and two new civilisations including the Swedes and Inca. Players build settlements, manage trade routes and command armies from the age of exploration through to the industrial era. Its Home City card system remains one of the most unique mechanics in the RTS genre.

name: Age of Empires IV: Anniversary Edition
synopsis: The Anniversary Edition of Age of Empires IV bundles the base game with post-launch civilisations including the Ottomans, Malians, Japanese and Byzantines alongside the Anniversary update's ranked seasons and improved AI. It is the most complete version of Relic's acclaimed modern reimagining of the classic RTS franchise.

name: Age of Empires: Definitive Edition
synopsis: A complete remaster of the 1997 original with 4K visuals, remastered music and all the Forgotten Empires expansion content. Players command ancient civilisations from the Stone Age through the Iron Age in historically inspired campaign missions and multiplayer. It is the essential starting point for the legendary Age of Empires franchise.

name: Age of History 3
synopsis: A turn-based grand strategy game covering the entire history of human civilisation from ancient times to the modern era. Players take control of any nation on a detailed world map and guide it through diplomacy, war and economic development across thousands of years. Its simple interface belies remarkable strategic depth.

name: Age of Mythology: Extended Edition
synopsis: The extended version of Ensemble's mythology-themed RTS classic adds the Tale of the Dragon campaign with Chinese civilisation alongside the original Greek, Egyptian and Norse factions. Each pantheon commands different gods who grant unique myth units and divine powers. It remains one of the most beloved entries in the RTS genre.

name: Age of Wonders: Planetfall
synopsis: A sci-fi 4X turn-based strategy game set in the ruins of a fallen intergalactic empire where six factions compete to reshape the galaxy. Players research technologies, conduct diplomacy, form alliances and command armies in turn-based tactical battles across planets and moons. Its faction customisation system allows enormous build variety.

name: Alan Wake
synopsis: Stephen King-inspired psychological horror thriller about bestselling novelist Alan Wake who arrives in Bright Falls, Washington only to find reality unravelling as the dark plot he cannot remember writing begins to come true. The game uses light as its primary mechanic — players must first burn away enemies' darkness before shooting them. Its Twin Peaks atmosphere is exceptional.

name: American Truck Simulator
synopsis: A trucking simulation game that lets players haul cargo across a beautifully recreated American Southwest spanning California, Nevada, Arizona and expanding states. The meditative drive across vast landscapes with a curated radio station is genuinely therapeutic and has attracted millions of players worldwide. Its active modding community continuously expands the map and vehicle roster.

name: Anno 2205
synopsis: A sci-fi city builder set in the 23rd century where players build lunar colonies and terrestrial megacities as humanity expands into space. Its three region types — continental, arctic and lunar — each require different resource management strategies. The game streamlined the Anno formula for accessibility but divided fans of the deeper economics in earlier entries.

name: Apex Legends
synopsis: EA and Respawn's free-to-play battle royale set in the Titanfall universe combines hero shooter abilities with a polished squad-based last-team-standing format. Its ping communication system was so revolutionary it has been copied by virtually every battle royale since launch. The game continues to receive new Legends, maps and seasonal content.

name: Arma 3
synopsis: The most realistic military simulation game available, featuring authentic infantry tactics, vehicle warfare and combined arms operations across a massive Mediterranean island. Its modding ecosystem has spawned Battle Royale as a genre (DayZ and PUBG both originated here), and its community creates content of extraordinary quality. Arma 3 rewards patience with unmatched tactical depth.

name: Assassin's Creed Brotherhood
synopsis: Continuing Ezio Auditore's story in Rome, Brotherhood introduced the revolutionary mechanic of recruiting and commanding an assassin brotherhood — sending trained Assassins on contracts or calling them to assist in combat. Its Italian Renaissance Rome is one of the series' most realised settings, and the multiplayer was uniquely clever. Often considered the high point of the Ezio trilogy.

name: Assassin's Creed IV Black Flag
synopsis: Players take the role of Welsh pirate Edward Kenway in the Golden Age of Piracy, commanding a ship called the Jackdaw across the Caribbean, raiding merchant vessels, harpooning whales and exploring uncharted islands. Its naval combat was so well-received that Ubisoft built entire games around it. Black Flag remains many players' favourite entry for its sheer sense of oceanic adventure.

name: Assassin's Creed Origins
synopsis: A dramatic reimagining of the series' foundations set in ancient Egypt, following Bayek of Siwa as he hunts those who killed his son and inadvertently founds the Brotherhood of Assassins. The RPG overhaul brought loot systems, skill trees and a vast open world spanning the Nile Delta to the Sinai. Its Discovery Tour mode turns the game into an interactive museum.

name: Assassin's Creed Valhalla
synopsis: Players lead Eivor, a Viking raider who leaves Norway for the shores of England in 872 AD to raid, build settlements and form alliances with Saxon kingdoms. At over 100 hours of content, it is the most expansive entry in the franchise. Its settlement building, orlog dice game, and alliance system represent the most personalised Assassin's Creed experience yet.

name: Back 4 Blood
synopsis: Spiritual successor to Left 4 Dead from the original creators, featuring squad-based co-op shooting against hordes of mutated zombies called Ridden. A card-based system lets players customise builds before each mission for different playstyles. Its stronger solo campaign compared to L4D and continued post-launch support built a dedicated community.

name: Baldur's Gate II: Enhanced Edition
synopsis: Beamdog's definitive edition of Black Isle's 2000 RPG masterpiece, widely regarded as the greatest CRPG ever made, following Gorion's Ward on an epic quest against the wizard Irenicus across Amn and beyond. The Enhanced Edition adds new companions, expanded content and full resolution support while preserving the original's extraordinary writing. It defined the CRPG genre.

name: Batman: Arkham Asylum GOTY Edition
synopsis: The game that proved superhero games could be extraordinary, trapping Batman in Arkham Asylum as the Joker takes over. Its Freeflow combat system, predator stealth sequences and Metroidvania exploration of the island were all genre-defining. Mark Hamill's Joker performance remains the definitive voice acting in a superhero game. A landmark moment for the medium.

name: Batman: Arkham City GOTY
synopsis: Widely considered the greatest superhero game ever made, Arkham City opens Gotham's slums as a prison mega-complex and delivers Batman's most epic adventure. Multiple villain storylines interweave across a brilliantly realised city, with a twist ending that genuinely shocked even comics veterans. The Catwoman DLC, Robin and Nightwing challenge maps made this the complete Batman experience.

name: Batman: Arkham Knight
synopsis: Rocksteady's conclusion to their Arkham trilogy introduces the Batmobile as a driveable tank across a fully realised Gotham City. The Arkham Knight's identity is a divisive but emotionally charged reveal. Despite the notorious PC port, the console versions delivered an emotionally resonant finale to one of gaming's greatest trilogies with spectacular Batman gameplay.

name: Batman: Arkham Origins
synopsis: A prequel to the Arkham series set during Batman's second year as a vigilante on Christmas Eve as eight assassins hunt him across Gotham City. Despite a weaker critical reception than Rocksteady's entries, its Black Mask/Joker story is genuinely compelling and Roger Craig Smith's Batman performance is excellent. The Deathstroke boss fight is the series' finest.

name: BeamNG.drive
synopsis: A soft-body physics vehicle simulation where cars deform with unprecedented realism in crashes that have to be seen to be believed. Every vehicle component flexes, bends and breaks independently based on real physics. Its sandbox nature — from slow traffic driving to catastrophic crash testing — has made it one of the most streamed games on YouTube. Early Access to full release.

name: Besiege
synopsis: A physics-based building game where players construct siege engines — catapults, trebuchets, cannons and fantastical war machines — from modular blocks to complete progressively harder medieval assault challenges. The emergent contraptions players devise range from elegant to hilariously overpowered. Its sandbox mode and mod support have produced extraordinary community creations.

name: Beyond Two Souls
synopsis: David Cage's cinematic narrative game starring Ellen Page and Willem Dafoe across the non-linear life story of Jodie Holmes, a young woman connected to a mysterious supernatural entity named Aiden. Its Hollywood production values and motion capture performances are extraordinary. The story's treatment of its themes divides critics but engages players emotionally throughout.

name: BioShock
synopsis: Andrew Ryan's libertarian underwater city of Rapture has collapsed into a civil war between genetically modified splicers, and you must survive using plasmids — genetic modifications giving powers like electricity and telekinesis — alongside conventional firearms. Its 'Would you kindly' twist is one of gaming's most celebrated narrative moments. A masterclass in environmental storytelling.

name: BioShock 2
synopsis: Players control Subject Delta, the first Big Daddy, returning to a decaying Rapture now under the control of collectivist philosopher Sofia Lamb. The Big Daddy perspective gives a fresh emotional dimension and the mechanics are refined over the original. Its competitive multiplayer set in Rapture's heyday was a fascinating experiment.

name: BioShock Infinite
synopsis: Pinkerton agent Booker DeWitt rescues the mysterious Elizabeth from the floating city of Columbia, a violent American exceptionalism utopia in the sky. The game's Tears mechanic and Elizabeth's ability to open portals to parallel realities build to one of the most mind-bending narrative endings in gaming. The Burial at Sea DLC ties the story back to Rapture.

name: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare
synopsis: A complete reimagining of the Modern Warfare sub-series featuring a realistic and visceral campaign following an SAS officer and CIA operative fighting terrorists in a fictional Middle Eastern country. The game launched Warzone and the new unified progression system across the franchise. Its gunfeel and weapon customisation via Gunsmith remain the gold standard for the genre.

name: Call of Duty: World at War
synopsis: Treyarch's gritty WWII shooter covers both the Pacific Theatre and the Eastern Front in campaigns of exceptional brutality — flame-throwers, samurai executions and the fall of Berlin. It introduced the iconic Nazi Zombies mode that spawned a franchise within a franchise. The multiplayer was the template for the Modern Warfare 2 system.

name: Call to Arms - Gates of Hell: Ostfront
Clone Drone in the Danger Zone
Company of Heroes
synopsis: Widely considered the greatest WWII RTS ever made, Company of Heroes revolutionised the genre with its physics-based destructible environments, realistic line-of-sight, and strategic use of cover. Every battle plays differently as players adapt to changing cover conditions. Its Normandy campaign and Eastern Front expansion remain genre benchmarks over two decades later.

name: Company of Heroes 3
synopsis: The long-awaited third entry in Relic's legendary WWII RTS franchise focuses on the Italian and North African campaigns, introducing a pausable operational map layer that wraps campaign battles in a strategic context. While praised for its Mediterranean setting and new factions, some fans felt it lacked the focused design of its predecessors at launch.

name: Counter-Strike
synopsis: Valve's complete overhaul of CS:GO uses the Source 2 engine to deliver the most technically refined version of counter-terrorism's defining competitive shooter. Smoke grenades now volumetrically react to gunfire and explosions. Server-side hit registration makes every shot feel precise. Despite transitional reception, it is the essential platform for competitive CS going forward.

name: Counter-Strike 2
synopsis: Valve's complete overhaul of CS:GO uses the Source 2 engine to deliver the most technically refined version of counter-terrorism's defining competitive shooter. Smoke grenades now volumetrically react to gunfire and explosions. Server-side hit registration makes every shot feel precise. Despite transitional reception, it is the essential platform for competitive CS going forward.

name: Crusader Kings III
synopsis: Paradox's grand strategy/RPG hybrid lets players control a medieval dynasty from 867 or 1066 AD, marrying, murdering, converting and conquering across centuries of European and Middle Eastern history. The game's focus on character relationships — murder plots, seductions, inheritance crises — makes it more RPG than traditional strategy. Its meme-worthy stories of dynastic catastrophes have made it one of gaming's most shared experiences.

name: Cult of the Lamb
synopsis: A possessed lamb escapes sacrifice and is tasked by a dark god with building a cult. Players run dungeons killing heretics in Binding of Isaac-style roguelite combat, then return to manage their cult village — building structures, performing sermons, feeding followers and punishing dissent. Its twin gameplay loops complement each other perfectly.

name: Desperados III
synopsis: Mimimi Games' Wild West spiritual successor to Shadow Tactics features five distinct specialists — including the legendary bounty hunter Cooper and the lethal McCoy — working together across massive sandbox levels demanding patience, planning and precision. Its Showdown Mode lets players pause and plan multi-kill chain reactions. Considered one of the finest tactics games of the decade.

name: Destiny 2
synopsis: Bungie's free-to-play shared world shooter has players take the role of Guardians protecting the last city on Earth from alien factions using supernatural Light abilities. Its legendary gunfeel — described as the best shooting in any game — powers seasonal content, raids and dungeons that have produced gaming's most passionate PvE community. The Forsaken and Witch Queen expansions are modern classics.

name: Deus Ex: Mankind Divided
synopsis: Jensen returns as an augmented Interpol agent navigating a Prague torn apart by human/augmented segregation in the aftermath of the Aug Incident, hunting a terrorist group while uncovering a deeper global conspiracy. Its dense level design rewards exploration and supports lethal or non-lethal approaches across multiple solutions. The Blade Runner-esque setting is beautifully realised.

name: Divinity: Original Sin 2
synopsis: Widely considered the greatest CRPG since Baldur's Gate 2, Divinity: Original Sin 2 features four-player co-op, fully voiced dialogue, a dynamic elemental reaction system, and extraordinary freedom — from the game's opening ship prison to its climactic godlike confrontation. Players create their own character or play as one of six origin characters with unique backstories. The Definitive Edition further polished an already exceptional game.

name: DOOM Eternal
synopsis: The definitive FPS experience with combat designed as an elaborate resource-management loop — chainsaw for ammo, glory kills for health, flame for armour — all while navigating and destroying waves of Hell's armies to a legendary Mick Gordon metal soundtrack. Its skill ceiling is extraordinarily high, rewarding mastery with the feeling of being unstoppable.

name: DRAGON BALL XENOVERSE 2
synopsis: Players create a custom Saiyan, Namekian, Frieza Race or other character who travels through Dragon Ball history as a Time Patroller protecting the timeline from changes. The hub world, character customisation and skill system create a surprisingly deep action RPG wrapped around 3D arena fighting. Seven years of DLC have made it the most content-rich Dragon Ball game ever released.

name: EA SPORTS FC 24
synopsis: The first annual football game under EA Sports' own FC branding following the split with FIFA, featuring HyperMotion V technology for more realistic player animations and the new PlayStyles system replacing chemistry cards. All the major leagues and competitions remain licensed. Its reception was mixed — praised for gameplay refinements but criticised for Ultimate Team monetisation practices.

name: EA SPORTS FC 25
synopsis: The second standalone EA Sports FC entry continues to refine the football simulation with updated Rush 5v5 mode and improved Rush club. All major international competitions including the Women's Champions League receive expanded coverage. Career Mode receives meaningful updates for manager and player paths alike.

name: EA SPORTS FIFA 23
synopsis: The final game under the historic FIFA licence, delivering both men's and women's World Cups alongside the standard club football experience. HyperMotion2 brought new animation fidelity for female player likenesses. Its swansong nature made it a landmark for the franchise's 30-year history before the EA Sports FC rebrand.

name: Elite Dangerous
synopsis: A 1:1 scale Milky Way simulation in the year 3309 where players can trade, fight, explore and mine across 400 billion procedurally generated star systems based on real astronomical data. The sheer sense of scale — flying across the observable galaxy takes real-world months — is unlike anything else. Its Odyssey expansion added on-foot planetary exploration.

name: Europa Universalis IV
synopsis: Paradox's grand strategy masterpiece covers 400 years of history from 1444 to 1821, letting players guide any nation through exploration, colonisation, trade, war and religious upheaval. Every run tells a completely different story — from building the Aztec Empire into a global superpower to surviving as tiny Venice. Its extraordinary depth has supported over a decade of expansion content.

name: Fallout: New Vegas
synopsis: Set in the Mojave Wasteland around post-apocalyptic Las Vegas, New Vegas follows a courier betrayed and left for dead who uncovers a battle for control of Hoover Dam between the NCR, Caesar's Legion and the mysterious Mr. House. Obsidian's nuanced faction writing, genuine RPG consequences and extraordinary companion characters make it the definitive Fallout experience. The 'Yes Man' route is still discussed today.

name: Far Cry 4
synopsis: Set in the fictional Himalayan nation of Kyrat, players join a civil war against charismatic dictator Pagan Min while uncovering the truth about their mother's past. The game introduced the most memorable villain in Far Cry history — Pagan Min, played brilliantly by Troy Baker — and expanded the series' co-op and wildlife systems. Taking the 10-minute ending remains one of gaming's best Easter eggs.

name: Far Cry 6
synopsis: Players take the role of guerrilla fighter Dani Rojas on the Caribbean island of Yara, overthrowing dictator Antón Castillo played by Giancarlo Esposito. The game introduces the most diverse and creative weapon crafting in the series — Supremo backpacks and Resolver weapons built from junk. Critics praised its world and characters while noting systemic repetition inherited from the series.

name: Football Manager 2024
synopsis: The most detailed football management simulation in history, placing players in the role of head coach for any professional club worldwide with accurate squad data, transfer market simulation, match engine tactics and press conference management. Its depth is extraordinary — some players spend thousands of hours perfecting a single save file. The 2024 edition brought improved pre-match and half-time tools.

name: Forza Horizon 4
synopsis: Set in a stunning recreation of the British countryside across dynamic seasons — spring, summer, autumn and winter — each changing the world weekly with new events, surfaces and challenges. Its shared open world means other players are visible as Drivatars competing in parallel. With over 450 cars and an enormous map spanning Edinburgh, the Lake District and Edinburgh, it remains a benchmark racing game.

name: Frostpunk
synopsis: A city-survival strategy game set in an apocalyptic ice age where players must keep the last city on Earth alive around its heat-generating Generator. Every decision carries moral weight — child labour, strict laws, hope vs discontent management — in a game that genuinely tests your ethics. The Book of Laws system creates some of gaming's most uncomfortable governing choices.

name: Gears 5
synopsis: The fifth Gears entry switches perspective to Kait Diaz as she investigates her connection to the Swarm and the Locust Queen. Its third act opens an enormous open world map traversed by Skiff vehicle, a dramatic departure for the series. The acclaimed Escape mode and Horde mode's tower defence improvements make this the most mechanically varied Gears game.

name: Grand Theft Auto IV: The Complete Edition
synopsis: Complete Edition of GTA IV bundling both the original game and the Lost and Damned and Ballad of Gay Tony DLC. Niko Bellic's immigrant story in Liberty City remains one of gaming's most mature narratives despite the base game's infamous PC performance issues. The DLC episodes stand as some of Rockstar's finest work.

name: Grand Theft Auto V Legacy
synopsis: The expanded and enhanced legacy edition of Rockstar's record-breaking 2013 masterpiece featuring three protagonists across the largest map in GTA history. Its billion-dollar GTA Online continues to receive updates years later. For PC players, the original 2015 version includes extensive mod support through the active modding community.

name: Hitman 2: Silent Assassin
synopsis: The sequel that defined the stealth assassination genre, following Agent 47 across missions in Sicily, Japan, India, Russia and Malaysia. Its disguise system and environmental storytelling set the template for all future Hitman games. The mission in a Russian gulag remains one of the most atmospheric stealth levels in gaming history.

name: Hitman: Blood Money
synopsis: Widely considered the series' peak until 2016, Blood Money gives Agent 47 maximum freedom to eliminate targets through elaborate accident kills, disguised infiltration and environmental storytelling. The New Orleans Mardi Gras mission and White House assassination are masterclasses of sandbox design. Its notoriety system tracks how effectively players conceal their kills.

name: HOT WHEELS UNLEASHED
synopsis: A faithful Hot Wheels adaptation that puts players behind 1:64 scale die-cast cars racing through the fantastically detailed miniature tracks in environments like college basements, skate parks and construction sites. Its 50+ authentic Hot Wheels cars are lovingly recreated, and the track builder allows extraordinary creativity. A genuine surprise hit that captured childhood nostalgia perfectly.

name: Idle Champions of the Forgotten Realms
Immortals Fenyx Rising
synopsis: A lighthearted Greek mythology open world adventure following Fenyx, a soldier on a divine quest to free the Greek gods from Typhon's curse. Its physics puzzles, combat and art style drew inevitable Breath of the Wild comparisons that undersold its own identity. Narrated by a bickering Prometheus and Zeus, its comedic tone is a refreshing change for the genre.

name: Iron Harvest
synopsis: A diesel-punk RTS set in the 1920s of the Scythe board game universe where giant mechs roam a war-scarred Europe. Players command three factions — Saxony Empire, Polania Republic and Rusviet — across single-player campaigns and competitive multiplayer with classic Company of Heroes-inspired cover and tactical depth.

name: Isonzo
synopsis: An atmospheric WWI tactical shooter focusing on the brutal Italian Front — a theatre often overlooked in gaming — with 60-player battles across historically detailed maps including the legendary Asiago Plateau and Monte Grappa. Its era-authentic weapons, attritional trench warfare mechanics and cinematic weather effects create the most immersive WWI shooter available.

name: Just Cause 3
synopsis: Rico Rodriguez returns to his Mediterranean homeland of Medici to liberate it from dictator General Di Ravello using an upgraded grapple hook, a wingsuit and unlimited explosives. The game is pure sandbox freedom — attaching gas canisters to soldiers, tethering vehicles together, and creating elaborate chain explosions across the beautiful Italian-inspired landscape. Glitches and DLC quality were criticised.

name: Kingdom Come: Deliverance
synopsis: An extraordinarily immersive first-person RPG set in 15th century Bohemia committed to historical authenticity — no magic, just medieval life. Henry of Skalitz starts with no combat skill, requiring players to genuinely train through practice. Its realistic swordfighting, lockpicking and alchemy systems are unlike anything in RPG history. The console version requires significant patience.

name: Knights of Honor II: Sovereign
synopsis: A medieval grand strategy game blending real-time kingdom management with simplified tactical battles across Europe and the Middle East. Players manage their Royal Court, send Knights on diplomatic or military missions, and grow their kingdom through trade, religion and warfare. Its streamlined approach makes grand strategy accessible without sacrificing the core fantasy of medieval empire building.

name: Mafia III: Definitive Edition
synopsis: Set in fictional New Bordeaux in 1968, Vietnam veteran Lincoln Clay dismantles the Italian mob that murdered his surrogate family in one of gaming's most boldly political stories about race and crime in the American South. Its authentic 1960s setting and excellent soundtrack are highlighted alongside repetitive mission design. The Definitive Edition includes all DLC.

name: Marvel's Avengers - The Definitive Edition
synopsis: Crystal Dynamics' ambitious attempt to create a live service Avengers game featuring original story campaigns for each hero alongside endgame progression systems. Its campaign following teenage Ms. Marvel and an aged AIM-conspiracy story received praise, but the looter shooter endgame failed to retain its player base. Servers closed in September 2023.

name: Metro 2033 Redux
synopsis: A stunning survival horror FPS set in the tunnels beneath post-nuclear Moscow, following Artyom on a desperate mission through the haunted Metro stations and irradiated surface. Bullets serve as both currency and ammunition. The Redux version delivers completely reworked AI, mechanics, and visual upgrades. Based on Dmitry Glukhovsky's acclaimed novel.

name: Metro Exodus
synopsis: Artyom and the Spartan Rangers flee the Moscow Metro aboard a steam locomotive in a cross-Russia journey through devastated open biomes — Volga, Caspian Desert, Taiga and Dead City. It dramatically expands Metro's world beyond the tunnels while retaining the survival horror atmosphere and human story. The Enhanced Edition adds ray-tracing of extraordinary visual quality.

name: Mortal Kombat 11
synopsis: NetherRealm's most polished fighting game delivers a fan-service story mode where past and present versions of the entire MK roster clash through time manipulation, alongside some of the most technically intricate fighting mechanics in the franchise. Its extensive customisation system for moves and gear allows players to build personal versions of every fighter.

name: Need for Speed: Heat
synopsis: NFS returns to its Underground roots in Palm City, a fictional Miami-inspired city where players race legitimately by day for prize money and evade corrupted cops in illegal night races for rep. Its day/night structure and deep car customisation are the strongest in the series in years. The cops are genuinely menacing and create tense pursuits.

name: Need for Speed: Rivals
synopsis: A seamless world racer where Racers and Cops exist simultaneously on the same map, creating emergent player-versus-player pursuits. Its AllDrive system meant single-player and multiplayer shared one open world. The car selection was strong and the sense of speed remained characteristic Criterion quality despite the studio's limited involvement.

name: Panzer Corps
synopsis: The definitive operational WWII hex-based strategy game, covering every major theatre with historical accuracy and extensive unit rosters from early Blitzkrieg through the fall of Berlin. Its prestige system lets players build a personalised veteran corps carried across the entire Grand Campaign. The Eastern Front expansion is considered one of the finest wargames ever produced.

name: Panzer Corps 2
synopsis: The definitive operational WWII hex-based strategy game, covering every major theatre with historical accuracy and extensive unit rosters from early Blitzkrieg through the fall of Berlin. Its prestige system lets players build a personalised veteran corps carried across the entire Grand Campaign. The Eastern Front expansion is considered one of the finest wargames ever produced.

name: PAYDAY 2
synopsis: One of gaming's longest-running co-op heist shooters, PAYDAY 2 lets players plan and execute elaborate bank robberies, drug operations, and museum heists across hundreds of available missions. Its build system with skills, masks, weapons and stealth-versus-loud approaches creates extraordinary replay value. Ten years of DLC and updates have built a dedicated crime community.

name: Portal
synopsis: Valve's revolutionary puzzle game challenges players to solve increasingly complex spatial puzzles using a portal gun that creates paired wormholes on surfaces. GLaDOS, the passive-aggressive AI managing the Aperture Science facility, delivers some of gaming's greatest writing. At under two hours, Portal is the most perfectly paced game Valve has ever made.

name: Prince of Persia: The Two Thrones
Quantum Break
synopsis: Remedy's ambitious blend of time-stop action gameplay and live-action TV drama interwoven between chapters, following protagonist Jack Joyce who gains time manipulation powers during a catastrophic experiment. Each player choice affects the parallel live-action episodes. Its visual style is exceptional and its story surprisingly moving despite the awkward format.

name: Red Dead Redemption 2
synopsis: Arthur Morgan's story as a loyal outlaw navigating an America being strangled by modernity is gaming's most emotionally resonant open world epic. Rockstar's attention to detail creates a world where strangers have their own stories. Its epilogue following John Marston is among the most poignant game endings ever written. The PS4 version is technically remarkable.

name: Rise of the Tomb Raider
synopsis: Lara Croft pursues the secret of immortality to Siberia and Syria against the fanatical Trinity organisation in Crystal Dynamics' finest Tomb Raider entry. Its crafting, bow-centric combat, and gorgeous environments represent the series at its best. The season pass content, particularly Blood Ties and Baba Yaga, expanded the experience substantially.

name: Sid Meier's Civilization VI
synopsis: The sixth Civilization introduces a groundbreaking district system that physically separates cities into specialized zones — science campuses, industrial zones, holy sites — requiring genuine urban planning. Leaders feature stylised animated portraits with era-specific attitudes. The Rise and Fall and Gathering Storm expansions added loyalty mechanics and climate change as strategic factors.

name: Sifu
synopsis: A third-person kung fu action game following a young martial artist on a revenge quest against five assassins who killed their father, aging with each death but never truly dying. Its combat system demands real mastery of parries, evades and chain attacks. On Platinum difficulty it is one of gaming's most demanding and rewarding skill-tests.

name: SOULCALIBUR VI
synopsis: The weapons-based fighting franchise returns to 1590 and retells the events of the original SoulCalibur with expanded lore and two new modes — Libra of Soul RPG and Soul Chronicle story campaigns. Geralt of Rivia from The Witcher is the standout guest character. Its Reversal Edge and Soul Charge mechanics create deeper defensive and offensive options.

name: South Park: The Stick of Truth
synopsis: The New Kid arrives in South Park and joins the children's fantasy LARPing game that quickly spirals into saving the world. Obsidian and Matt Stone/Trey Parker's collaboration produces a game that looks and feels exactly like playing an episode of the show. Its crude humour is extraordinary in context, and its turn-based RPG mechanics are genuinely satisfying.

name: Steel Division: Normandy 44
synopsis: An operational WWII RTS focusing on the Normandy campaign with extraordinary historical accuracy — real unit designations, equipment and doctrine. Its three-phase battle system that divides each engagement into A, B and C phases creates dynamic front-line management. Its depth approaches wargame territory while remaining playable for RTS veterans.

name: Tales from the Borderlands
synopsis: Telltale's finest work, following con artist Fiona and Hyperion corporate lackey Rhys whose heist-gone-wrong story across Pandora is narrated in flashback to an intimidating masked figure. Its humour is extraordinary, its character writing emotional and its ending genuinely moving. Many players consider it better than any of the mainline Borderlands entries.

name: TEKKEN 7
synopsis: The seventh mainline entry concludes the saga of the Mishima blood war with a cinematic story mode revealing Kazuya's backstory and bringing the conflict to a brutal conclusion. Its Rage Drive and Rage Art mechanics create dramatic comeback potential. Guest characters including Noctis and Geese Howard were controversial but kept the roster discussions lively.

name: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Game of the Year Edition
synopsis: The fourth Elder Scrolls follows the player character escaping prison to protect the heir of the Septim bloodline as Oblivion gates spill daedra across Tamriel. Its fully voiced world, radiant AI and extraordinary mod support made it one of PC gaming's landmark experiences. The Dark Brotherhood assassination questline remains Bethesda's finest narrative design.

name: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim Special Edition
synopsis: The Special Edition remaster of gaming's most ubiquitous RPG with improved visuals, mod support on console and all three DLC included — Dawnguard, Hearthfire and Dragonborn. Players create the Dragonborn, who alone can absorb dragon souls and use the Thu'um to shout enemies into oblivion. Its enduring modding community has extended its life across 15 years.

name: The Sims 4
synopsis: EA's life simulation franchise went free-to-play in 2022, making its vast library of expansion packs, game packs and stuff packs accessible to everyone. Players create and control virtual people across homes, careers and relationships with extraordinary creativity tools. Its modding community has produced content of remarkable quality including full overhauls.

name: The Witcher 2: Assassins of Kings Enhanced Edition
synopsis: The pivotal second Witcher game established CD Projekt Red as a premier RPG studio with branching story paths that create completely different second acts depending on who Geralt sides with. Its political intrigue, morally complex characters and exceptional writing laid the foundation for The Witcher 3. The Enhanced Edition is the definitive version.

name: Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege
synopsis: The definitive tactical multiplayer shooter where asymmetric attack/defend matches, destructible environments and unique Operator abilities create emergent situations impossible to plan for. Its 60+ Operators — each with distinct gadgets — create a meta that continuously evolves across seasons. From a rough launch it became one of the longest-running active esports titles.

name: Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege - Test Server
Tom Clancy's The Division
synopsis: An open world third-person shooter set in a quarantined Manhattan weeks after a virus-covered dollar bill outbreak, where Division sleeper agents attempt to restore order amid civil chaos. Its atmospheric snow-covered recreation of New York and the Dark Zone PvPvE mechanic were genuinely innovative. Post-launch content substantially improved the base game.

name: Total War: ATTILA
synopsis: Set during the twilight of the Roman Empire, players can either defend civilisation against the incoming Hunnic invasion or play as Attila himself to bring fire and sword across Europe. Its burning mechanics, apocalyptic migrations and challenging Roman survival scenarios make it one of the most atmospheric and difficult Total War entries.

name: Total War: MEDIEVAL II - Definitive Edition
Total War: ROME II - Emperor Edition
Total War: ROME REMASTERED
synopsis: A remaster of the 2004 original that launched the modern Total War era, updating the game to 4K with improved models while preserving the strategic and tactical essence that made it legendary. Players guide one of three Roman families or barbarian factions to Mediterranean domination. The remaster's fidelity to the original divides fans expecting more substantial changes.

name: Two Point Hospital
synopsis: A spiritual successor to Theme Hospital from the original team, placing players in charge of increasingly absurd hospitals treating fictional diseases like 'Boggled Mind' and 'Fake Plague'. Its dry British humour, satire-filled radio station and genuine management depth make it one of the most delightful simulation games of the decade. The Creative Hospital DLC expanded building tools significantly.

name: Unity of Command II
synopsis: The cleanest and most elegant WWII wargame available, focusing on supply lines and encirclement as the core strategic concepts through a comprehensive WW2 campaign spanning Rommel's Tunisia to the fall of Berlin. Its streamlined design eliminates tedium while preserving authentic operational decisions. Considered the gateway wargame for strategy players seeking historical depth.

name: Victoria II
synopsis: Paradox's 19th century grand strategy simulation covering industrialisation, colonialism, class politics and nationalism from 1836 to 1936. Its pop system simulates every citizen's political beliefs, job and wealth creating emergent social revolutions. Despite its age and interface limitations, it remains the most sophisticated simulation of the Victorian era in any medium.

name: Wolfenstein: The New Order
synopsis: A cinematic FPS set in an alternate 1960s where the Nazis won WWII, following BJ Blazkowicz's one-man resistance against an Orwellian fascist regime. MachineGames combined extraordinary action set-pieces with surprisingly thoughtful character writing that made BJ one of gaming's most compelling protagonists. Its sequel The New Colossus was even more ambitious.

name: Wolfenstein: The Old Blood
synopsis: A standalone prequel to The New Order following BJ Blazkowicz infiltrating Castle Wolfenstein in an homage to the original games. Its two-part structure — escape from Wolfenstein and a supernatural zombie outbreak — makes for a shorter but entertaining Wolfenstein experience. The stealth mechanics and dual pipe weapons are highlights.

name: XCOM 2
synopsis: Set 20 years after XCOM's failure, humanity lives under alien occupation as a propaganda-backed utopia and players command guerrilla forces aboard the Avenger mobile base. Its pod-based ambush system, timer-based missions and brutal permanent death for soldiers create extraordinary tension. War of the Chosen adds three nemesis faction aliens who dynamically hunt your squad.

name: XCOM: Chimera Squad
synopsis: A standalone spinoff following XCOM: 2's aftermath as human and alien agents form Chimera Squad to maintain peace in the integrated City 31. Breach mode — coordinated simultaneous entry through windows and doors — creates the series' most spectacular tactical moments. Its fixed named characters with unique abilities replace the traditional soldier customisation.
